title: Partnerships
subject: Partnerships
summary: Partnerships covers business co-ownership, partner duties, authority, liability, dissociation, dissolution, LLPs, and limited partnerships.

sections.title: Big Picture
body: Partnership law is about people running a business together.
The main MBE questions are usually:
Who is liable for the partnership’s contracts or torts?
What rights and duties do partners owe each other?
What happens when a partner leaves?
What happens when the partnership ends?
What changes if it is an LLP or limited partnership?
A partnership can exist even if the people never say:
The law looks at what they actually did.

blocks.text: Partnership law is about people running a business together.
The main MBE questions are usually:
Who is liable for the partnership’s contracts or torts?
What rights and duties do partners owe each other?
What happens when a partner leaves?
What happens when the partnership ends?
What changes if it is an LLP or limited partnership?
A partnership can exist even if the people never say:
The law looks at what they actually did.

sections.title: 1. What Is a Partnership?
body: An association of two or more persons who intend to carry on a for-profit business as co-owners.
Maya and Leo agree to run a small coffee stand together.
Even if they never use the word “partnership,” they probably formed one.
Partnership = two or more people running a for-profit business together as co-owners.

blocks.text: An association of two or more persons who intend to carry on a for-profit business as co-owners.
Maya and Leo agree to run a small coffee stand together.
Even if they never use the word “partnership,” they probably formed one.
Partnership = two or more people running a for-profit business together as co-owners.

sections.title: 2. Who Can Be a Partner?
body: A person must have capacity to contract.
A corporation and an individual can form a partnership together.
A partner can be a human or a legal entity.

blocks.text: A person must have capacity to contract.
A corporation and an individual can form a partnership together.
A partner can be a human or a legal entity.

sections.title: 3. Intent to Form a Partnership
body: The partners do not need specific intent to form a partnership.
They only need intent to carry on a business together as co-owners.
“We are not partners. We are just working together.”
But they split profits, share control, and operate a business together.
The law may still say they are partners.
You do not need to intend the label “partnership.” You need to intend the business relationship.

blocks.text: The partners do not need specific intent to form a partnership.
They only need intent to carry on a business together as co-owners.
“We are not partners. We are just working together.”
But they split profits, share control, and operate a business together.
The law may still say they are partners.
You do not need to intend the label “partnership.” You need to intend the business relationship.

sections.title: 4. Written Agreement Not Required
body: A general partnership does not usually need a written agreement.
It can be created by conduct.
If the partnership agreement cannot be performed within one year, the Statute of Frauds may require a writing.
Maya and Leo orally agree to run a tutoring business indefinitely.
General partnerships can be formed informally.

blocks.text: A general partnership does not usually need a written agreement.
It can be created by conduct.
If the partnership agreement cannot be performed within one year, the Statute of Frauds may require a writing.
Maya and Leo orally agree to run a tutoring business indefinitely.
General partnerships can be formed informally.

sections.title: 5. Profit Sharing
body: Sharing profits creates a rebuttable presumption that a partnership exists.
Maya and Leo run a snack stand.
Sharing gross returns does not automatically create a partnership.
Leo operates a vending-machine business there.
Leo gives Maya 10% of gross sales as rent.
That does not automatically make Maya a partner.
Sharing profits suggests partnership. Sharing gross receipts does not.

blocks.text: Sharing profits creates a rebuttable presumption that a partnership exists.
Maya and Leo run a snack stand.
Sharing gross returns does not automatically create a partnership.
Leo operates a vending-machine business there.
Leo gives Maya 10% of gross sales as rent.
That does not automatically make Maya a partner.
Sharing profits suggests partnership. Sharing gross receipts does not.

sections.title: 6. Profit-Sharing Exceptions
body: Profit sharing does not create a partnership presumption if the payment is really one of these things.
Goodwill payments from sale of a business
Maya lends Leo money for his bakery.
Leo promises to repay Maya with 10% of bakery profits until the loan is paid.
Maya is probably a creditor, not a partner.
Profit sharing creates partnership presumption unless it is really DRAWING.

blocks.text: Profit sharing does not create a partnership presumption if the payment is really one of these things.
Goodwill payments from sale of a business
Maya lends Leo money for his bakery.
Leo promises to repay Maya with 10% of bakery profits until the loan is paid.
Maya is probably a creditor, not a partner.
Profit sharing creates partnership presumption unless it is really DRAWING.

sections.title: 7. Joint Ownership of Property Is Not Enough
body: Owning property together does not automatically create a partnership.
Even sharing profits from property use is not enough by itself.
Maya and Leo own a duplex together and split rental income.
That alone does not make them partners.
You need evidence they are running a business as co-owners with shared control.
Owning property together is not the same as running a business together.

blocks.text: Owning property together does not automatically create a partnership.
Even sharing profits from property use is not enough by itself.
Maya and Leo own a duplex together and split rental income.
That alone does not make them partners.
You need evidence they are running a business as co-owners with shared control.
Owning property together is not the same as running a business together.

sections.title: 8. Shared Control Matters
body: Courts look at whether the parties share control over the business.
Maya and Leo both decide pricing, suppliers, hours, advertising, and hiring for a small gym.
Maya only receives rent from Leo’s gym.
Maya has no control over the gym.
That does not look like partnership.
Partnership needs business co-control, not passive ownership.

blocks.text: Courts look at whether the parties share control over the business.
Maya and Leo both decide pricing, suppliers, hours, advertising, and hiring for a small gym.
Maya only receives rent from Leo’s gym.
Maya has no control over the gym.
That does not look like partnership.
Partnership needs business co-control, not passive ownership.

sections.title: 9. Subpartnership
body: A subpartnership is an agreement between a partner and a third party to share that partner’s profits.
The third party does not become a partner in the main partnership.
Maya is a partner in a bakery partnership.
“I’ll give you half of whatever profits I receive from the bakery.”
Nora does not become a bakery partner.
Nora only has a contract claim against Maya.
Subpartner shares one partner’s profits, not partnership power.

blocks.text: A subpartnership is an agreement between a partner and a third party to share that partner’s profits.
The third party does not become a partner in the main partnership.
Maya is a partner in a bakery partnership.
“I’ll give you half of whatever profits I receive from the bakery.”
Nora does not become a bakery partner.
Nora only has a contract claim against Maya.
Subpartner shares one partner’s profits, not partnership power.

sections.title: 10. Joint Venture
body: A joint venture is usually a partnership for a specific limited purpose.
Courts often apply partnership rules if the venture is business-related.
Maya and Leo agree to buy, renovate, and resell one apartment building.
That may be a joint venture.
Joint venture = partnership-like business project for a limited purpose.

blocks.text: A joint venture is usually a partnership for a specific limited purpose.
Courts often apply partnership rules if the venture is business-related.
Maya and Leo agree to buy, renovate, and resell one apartment building.
That may be a joint venture.
Joint venture = partnership-like business project for a limited purpose.

sections.title: 11. Partner by Estoppel / Purported Partner
body: A person can be liable as a partner if:
They represent themselves as a partner, or consent to being represented as a partner;
A third party reasonably relies on that representation; and
“Leo is my partner in this restaurant.”
Leo hears this and says nothing, even though he knows Maya is using that statement to get credit.
The supplier relies and extends credit.
Leo may be liable as a purported partner.
If you let people think you are a partner and they rely, you may be treated like one.

blocks.text: A person can be liable as a partner if:
They represent themselves as a partner, or consent to being represented as a partner;
A third party reasonably relies on that representation; and
“Leo is my partner in this restaurant.”
Leo hears this and says nothing, even though he knows Maya is using that statement to get credit.
The supplier relies and extends credit.
Leo may be liable as a purported partner.
If you let people think you are a partner and they rely, you may be treated like one.

sections.title: 12. No Duty to Deny Without Consent
body: If someone falsely calls you a partner without your consent, you generally do not have a duty to deny it.
Maya tells people Leo is her partner.
Leo does not know Maya is saying this.
Leo is usually not liable just because he failed to deny it.
No consent to being held out = usually no partner-by-estoppel liability.

blocks.text: If someone falsely calls you a partner without your consent, you generally do not have a duty to deny it.
Maya tells people Leo is her partner.
Leo does not know Maya is saying this.
Leo is usually not liable just because he failed to deny it.
No consent to being held out = usually no partner-by-estoppel liability.

sections.title: 13. Public Holding Out
body: If the representation that someone is a partner is made publicly, the purported partner may be liable even if they did not know the specific third party saw it.
Leo knowingly lets his name appear on the restaurant’s website as “partner.”
A supplier sees the website and extends credit.
Publicly letting yourself look like a partner can create liability.

blocks.text: If the representation that someone is a partner is made publicly, the purported partner may be liable even if they did not know the specific third party saw it.
Leo knowingly lets his name appear on the restaurant’s website as “partner.”
A supplier sees the website and extends credit.
Publicly letting yourself look like a partner can create liability.

sections.title: 14. Partnership as a Separate Entity
body: A partnership is a legal entity separate from its partners.
“Maya & Leo Fitness Partnership” can own gym equipment.
The individual partners do not personally own each item.
Partnership is its own legal entity, but partners may still be personally liable.

blocks.text: A partnership is a legal entity separate from its partners.
“Maya & Leo Fitness Partnership” can own gym equipment.
The individual partners do not personally own each item.
Partnership is its own legal entity, but partners may still be personally liable.

sections.title: 15. Partnership Agreement Controls
body: The partnership agreement generally controls the relationship among:
Some duties cannot be fully eliminated, like:
Partnership agreement is usually the law of the partnership.

blocks.text: The partnership agreement generally controls the relationship among:
Some duties cannot be fully eliminated, like:
Partnership agreement is usually the law of the partnership.

sections.title: 16. Partner as Agent
body: Each partner is an agent of the partnership for partnership business.
That means a partner can bind the partnership in contracts if the partner has authority.
Maya and Leo run a pizza shop.
Maya orders cheese from a supplier in the ordinary course of business.

blocks.text: Each partner is an agent of the partnership for partnership business.
That means a partner can bind the partnership in contracts if the partner has authority.
Maya and Leo run a pizza shop.
Maya orders cheese from a supplier in the ordinary course of business.

sections.title: 17. Partner Fiduciary Duties
body: Partners owe fiduciary duties to the partnership and to each other.
Partners must be loyal, careful, and fair.

blocks.text: Partners owe fiduciary duties to the partnership and to each other.
Partners must be loyal, careful, and fair.

sections.title: 18. Duty of Loyalty
body: Use partnership property for personal benefit
Maya and Leo run a catering partnership.
Maya secretly takes a catering job for herself that should have gone to the partnership.
Maya likely breached the duty of loyalty.
Duty of loyalty = do not secretly benefit yourself at the partnership’s expense.

blocks.text: Use partnership property for personal benefit
Maya and Leo run a catering partnership.
Maya secretly takes a catering job for herself that should have gone to the partnership.
Maya likely breached the duty of loyalty.
Duty of loyalty = do not secretly benefit yourself at the partnership’s expense.

sections.title: 19. Duty of Loyalty Cannot Be Eliminated
body: The partnership agreement cannot completely eliminate the duty of loyalty.
But it may define certain activities that are allowed, if not unreasonable.
Partners may own separate coffee shops outside the city.
That may be allowed if reasonable and clearly agreed.
The agreement may allow disinterested partners to approve a transaction after full disclosure.
Loyalty cannot be erased, but some conflicts can be approved.

blocks.text: The partnership agreement cannot completely eliminate the duty of loyalty.
But it may define certain activities that are allowed, if not unreasonable.
Partners may own separate coffee shops outside the city.
That may be allowed if reasonable and clearly agreed.
The agreement may allow disinterested partners to approve a transaction after full disclosure.
Loyalty cannot be erased, but some conflicts can be approved.

sections.title: 20. Duty of Care
body: He knowingly ignores tax filing obligations.
That may breach the duty of care.
Ordinary bad judgment is usually not enough.
Duty of care in partnership = avoid really careless or wrongful conduct.

blocks.text: He knowingly ignores tax filing obligations.
That may breach the duty of care.
Ordinary bad judgment is usually not enough.
Duty of care in partnership = avoid really careless or wrongful conduct.

sections.title: 21. Good Faith and Fair Dealing
body: Partners must act honestly and fairly when exercising partnership rights.
Maya uses a technical voting rule to freeze Leo out for no real business reason.
That may violate good faith and fair dealing.
Even when using your rights, do not play dirty.

blocks.text: Partners must act honestly and fairly when exercising partnership rights.
Maya uses a technical voting rule to freeze Leo out for no real business reason.
That may violate good faith and fair dealing.
Even when using your rights, do not play dirty.

sections.title: 22. Timing of Fiduciary Duties
body: Prospective partners usually do not owe partnership fiduciary duties yet.
But they still cannot commit fraud.
The duty not to compete generally ends after dissociation.
Partners winding up the business still owe duties related to winding up.
Full fiduciary duties usually start when partnership starts and change when partner leaves.

blocks.text: Prospective partners usually do not owe partnership fiduciary duties yet.
But they still cannot commit fraud.
The duty not to compete generally ends after dissociation.
Partners winding up the business still owe duties related to winding up.
Full fiduciary duties usually start when partnership starts and change when partner leaves.

sections.title: 23. Profits and Losses
body: Partners can agree to split profits and losses however they want.
Profits and losses are shared equally.
Losses are shared the same way as profits.
Agreement controls. If silent, equal shares. If only profits stated, losses follow profits.

blocks.text: Partners can agree to split profits and losses however they want.
Profits and losses are shared equally.
Losses are shared the same way as profits.
Agreement controls. If silent, equal shares. If only profits stated, losses follow profits.

sections.title: 24. Partner’s Account
body: Each partner has an account with the partnership.
Maya’s account is credited with her share.
Partner’s account = running balance of what partner put in and is owed.

blocks.text: Each partner has an account with the partnership.
Maya’s account is credited with her share.
Partner’s account = running balance of what partner put in and is owed.

sections.title: 25. Partner Loans to Partnership
body: A partner can loan money to the partnership.
The partner is treated like a creditor for that loan.
Leo loans the partnership $10,000 to pay rent.
Leo is a creditor for that $10,000 loan.
Partner contribution is capital. Partner loan is debt.

blocks.text: A partner can loan money to the partnership.
The partner is treated like a creditor for that loan.
Leo loans the partnership $10,000 to pay rent.
Leo is a creditor for that $10,000 loan.
Partner contribution is capital. Partner loan is debt.

sections.title: 26. Distributions
body: A partner cannot automatically demand a distribution of profits.
A partner’s account can be credited with profits, but cash does not have to be distributed unless agreed.
Maya cannot necessarily demand her cash share immediately.
A partner cannot demand specific partnership property.
Profit credit does not always mean cash payout now.

blocks.text: A partner cannot automatically demand a distribution of profits.
A partner’s account can be credited with profits, but cash does not have to be distributed unless agreed.
Maya cannot necessarily demand her cash share immediately.
A partner cannot demand specific partnership property.
Profit credit does not always mean cash payout now.

sections.title: 27. Partnership Interest
body: A partner’s partnership interest is personal property.
Even if the partnership owns land, the partner’s partnership interest is still personal property.
Partner owns a personal property interest, not pieces of partnership assets.

blocks.text: A partner’s partnership interest is personal property.
Even if the partnership owns land, the partner’s partnership interest is still personal property.
Partner owns a personal property interest, not pieces of partnership assets.

sections.title: 28. Transfer of Partnership Interest
body: A partner can transfer their partnership interest to a third party.
But the transferee usually gets only the financial rights.
Maya transfers her right to partnership distributions to Nora.
Nora does not become a partner or get management power.
Transfer of partnership interest transfers money rights, not partner status.

blocks.text: A partner can transfer their partnership interest to a third party.
But the transferee usually gets only the financial rights.
Maya transfers her right to partnership distributions to Nora.
Nora does not become a partner or get management power.
Transfer of partnership interest transfers money rights, not partner status.

sections.title: 29. Transfer Does Not Automatically Dissolve Partnership
body: A partner transferring their partnership interest does not automatically dissolve the partnership or dissociate the partner.
Leo assigns his distribution rights to Nora.
Leo may still remain a partner unless another dissociation event occurs.
Assignment of profits does not automatically make the assignee a partner.

blocks.text: A partner transferring their partnership interest does not automatically dissolve the partnership or dissociate the partner.
Leo assigns his distribution rights to Nora.
Leo may still remain a partner unless another dissociation event occurs.
Assignment of profits does not automatically make the assignee a partner.

sections.title: 30. Charging Order
body: A partner’s personal creditor can reach the partner’s partnership interest only through a charging order.
It gives the creditor a lien on the partner’s distribution rights.
Leo personally owes a creditor money.
The creditor cannot take partnership equipment.
The creditor must get a charging order against Leo’s partnership interest.
Partner creditor gets distributions, not partnership property.

blocks.text: A partner’s personal creditor can reach the partner’s partnership interest only through a charging order.
It gives the creditor a lien on the partner’s distribution rights.
Leo personally owes a creditor money.
The creditor cannot take partnership equipment.
The creditor must get a charging order against Leo’s partnership interest.
Partner creditor gets distributions, not partnership property.

sections.title: 31. Partnership Property
body: Property acquired by the partnership belongs to the partnership, not individual partners.
The partnership buys ovens for a bakery.
The ovens belong to the partnership entity, not to Maya or Leo personally.
Partner Cannot Transfer Partnership Property Personally
A partner cannot sell their personal “share” of a specific partnership asset.
Partnership property belongs to the partnership.

blocks.text: Property acquired by the partnership belongs to the partnership, not individual partners.
The partnership buys ovens for a bakery.
The ovens belong to the partnership entity, not to Maya or Leo personally.
Partner Cannot Transfer Partnership Property Personally
A partner cannot sell their personal “share” of a specific partnership asset.
Partnership property belongs to the partnership.

sections.title: 32. When Is Property Partnership Property?
body: Titled in partner’s name as partner
Maya buys a delivery van using partnership funds.
Even if title is in Maya’s name, it is presumed partnership property.
Partnership money usually means partnership property.

blocks.text: Titled in partner’s name as partner
Maya buys a delivery van using partnership funds.
Even if title is in Maya’s name, it is presumed partnership property.
Partnership money usually means partnership property.

sections.title: 33. New Partner
body: A new partner can join only with consent of all existing partners unless the agreement says otherwise.
Unless the agreement says otherwise, both Maya and Leo must consent.
New partner usually requires unanimous consent.

blocks.text: A new partner can join only with consent of all existing partners unless the agreement says otherwise.
Unless the agreement says otherwise, both Maya and Leo must consent.
New partner usually requires unanimous consent.

sections.title: 34. Management Rights
body: Each partner has equal rights in management unless the partnership agreement says otherwise.
A majority of partners can decide ordinary business matters.
Maya, Leo, and Nora run a bookstore.
A majority vote to buy more bookshelves.
Matters outside the ordinary course require unanimous consent.
The bookstore partners want to sell the entire business.
That likely requires all partners to agree.
Ordinary business = majority. Extraordinary business = unanimous.

blocks.text: Each partner has equal rights in management unless the partnership agreement says otherwise.
A majority of partners can decide ordinary business matters.
Maya, Leo, and Nora run a bookstore.
A majority vote to buy more bookshelves.
Matters outside the ordinary course require unanimous consent.
The bookstore partners want to sell the entire business.
That likely requires all partners to agree.
Ordinary business = majority. Extraordinary business = unanimous.

sections.title: 35. Partner Authority in Ordinary Business
body: A partner acting alone usually has actual authority for ordinary partnership business.
Leo buys flour for the bakery partnership.
A partner may lack actual authority if they know other partners would disagree or consultation is appropriate.
One partner can usually do ordinary business.

blocks.text: A partner acting alone usually has actual authority for ordinary partnership business.
Leo buys flour for the bakery partnership.
A partner may lack actual authority if they know other partners would disagree or consultation is appropriate.
One partner can usually do ordinary business.

sections.title: 36. Partner Compensation
body: A partner is usually not entitled to salary for partnership work unless:
The partnership agreement says so, or
The partner is winding up the partnership and earns reasonable compensation.
Maya works 60 hours per week at the partnership café.
Unless there is an agreement, Maya does not automatically get a salary.
She gets her share of profits.
Partners get profits, not salary, unless agreed.

blocks.text: A partner is usually not entitled to salary for partnership work unless:
The partnership agreement says so, or
The partner is winding up the partnership and earns reasonable compensation.
Maya works 60 hours per week at the partnership café.
Unless there is an agreement, Maya does not automatically get a salary.
She gets her share of profits.
Partners get profits, not salary, unless agreed.

sections.title: 37. Reimbursement and Indemnification
body: The partnership must reimburse a partner for proper payments or advances made for partnership business.
Leo personally pays $2,000 to repair partnership equipment.
The partnership must protect a partner from personal liabilities incurred in ordinary partnership business.
Maya signs a proper supply contract for the partnership and becomes personally liable.
If partner pays or becomes liable for partnership business, partnership usually reimburses or indemnifies.

blocks.text: The partnership must reimburse a partner for proper payments or advances made for partnership business.
Leo personally pays $2,000 to repair partnership equipment.
The partnership must protect a partner from personal liabilities incurred in ordinary partnership business.
Maya signs a proper supply contract for the partnership and becomes personally liable.
If partner pays or becomes liable for partnership business, partnership usually reimburses or indemnifies.

sections.title: 38. Use of Partnership Property
body: A partner may use partnership property only for partnership purposes.
Leo uses the partnership delivery van for a personal vacation.
Leo may owe the partnership for that benefit.
Partnership property is for partnership business.

blocks.text: A partner may use partnership property only for partnership purposes.
Leo uses the partnership delivery van for a personal vacation.
Leo may owe the partnership for that benefit.
Partnership property is for partnership business.

sections.title: 39. Access to Partnership Records
body: Partners have the right to inspect and copy partnership records during business hours.
The agreement cannot unreasonably restrict access.
Maya asks to see the partnership bank statements.
The partnership must provide reasonable access.
Partner has a right to know what is happening in the partnership.

blocks.text: Partners have the right to inspect and copy partnership records during business hours.
The agreement cannot unreasonably restrict access.
Maya asks to see the partnership bank statements.
The partnership must provide reasonable access.
Partner has a right to know what is happening in the partnership.

sections.title: 40. Duty to Provide Information
body: Partners and the partnership must provide:
Information reasonably needed for partner rights and duties, even without demand; and
Leo learns the partnership is about to lose a major client.
He must disclose that to the other partners.
Partners must share important partnership information.

blocks.text: Partners and the partnership must provide:
Information reasonably needed for partner rights and duties, even without demand; and
Leo learns the partnership is about to lose a major client.
He must disclose that to the other partners.
Partners must share important partnership information.

sections.title: 41. Lawsuits by Partnership or Partner
body: A partnership may sue a partner for breach of agreement or breach of duty.
Partner Can Sue Partnership or Other Partner
A partner may sue to enforce rights under RUPA or the partnership agreement.
One partner may sue another partner for personal injury caused by that partner’s tort.
Leo negligently drops heavy equipment on Maya during partnership work.
Leo’s negligence is not imputed to Maya to bar her claim.
Partners can sue each other when rights or duties are violated.

blocks.text: A partnership may sue a partner for breach of agreement or breach of duty.
Partner Can Sue Partnership or Other Partner
A partner may sue to enforce rights under RUPA or the partnership agreement.
One partner may sue another partner for personal injury caused by that partner’s tort.
Leo negligently drops heavy equipment on Maya during partnership work.
Leo’s negligence is not imputed to Maya to bar her claim.
Partners can sue each other when rights or duties are violated.

sections.title: 42. Dissociation
body: A partner stops being associated with carrying on the partnership business.
A partner can dissociate voluntarily or involuntarily.
Dissociation = partner leaves the partnership relationship.

blocks.text: A partner stops being associated with carrying on the partnership business.
A partner can dissociate voluntarily or involuntarily.
Dissociation = partner leaves the partnership relationship.

sections.title: 43. Partner’s Power to Dissociate
body: A partner has the power to dissociate at any time.
Even if the dissociation is wrongful, it still happens.
Leo promises to stay in the partnership for five years.
Leo has dissociated, but the dissociation may be wrongful.
Partner can always leave, but may have to pay for leaving wrongly.

blocks.text: A partner has the power to dissociate at any time.
Even if the dissociation is wrongful, it still happens.
Leo promises to stay in the partnership for five years.
Leo has dissociated, but the dissociation may be wrongful.
Partner can always leave, but may have to pay for leaving wrongly.

sections.title: 44. Wrongful Dissociation
body: For an open-ended partnership, dissociation is wrongful only if it breaches the partnership agreement.
Dissociation is wrongful if, before the term ends or undertaking is complete, the partner:
Wrongfully terminates as an entity partner
Maya and Leo form a partnership to renovate one building.
Leo quits halfway through without justification.
Leaving early from a term/project partnership can be wrongful.

blocks.text: For an open-ended partnership, dissociation is wrongful only if it breaches the partnership agreement.
Dissociation is wrongful if, before the term ends or undertaking is complete, the partner:
Wrongfully terminates as an entity partner
Maya and Leo form a partnership to renovate one building.
Leo quits halfway through without justification.
Leaving early from a term/project partnership can be wrongful.

sections.title: 45. Effect of Wrongful Dissociation
body: A wrongfully dissociated partner is liable for damages caused by the dissociation.
Leo wrongfully leaves a restaurant partnership right before opening night.
The partnership loses money because it must hire emergency replacement management.

blocks.text: A wrongfully dissociated partner is liable for damages caused by the dissociation.
Leo wrongfully leaves a restaurant partnership right before opening night.
The partnership loses money because it must hire emergency replacement management.

sections.title: 46. Dissociated Partner’s Management Rights
body: A dissociated partner generally loses the right to participate in management.
Maya cannot keep voting on ordinary business decisions.

blocks.text: A dissociated partner generally loses the right to participate in management.
Maya cannot keep voting on ordinary business decisions.

sections.title: 47. Dissociated Partner’s Duties
body: Duty not to compete usually ends.
Other duties end for post-dissociation events.
Duties may continue for winding up if partner participates.
After Leo leaves the partnership, Leo may start a competing business unless the agreement validly restricts him.
After dissociation, noncompete duty usually ends.

blocks.text: Duty not to compete usually ends.
Other duties end for post-dissociation events.
Duties may continue for winding up if partner participates.
After Leo leaves the partnership, Leo may start a competing business unless the agreement validly restricts him.
After dissociation, noncompete duty usually ends.

sections.title: 48. Buyout of Dissociated Partner
body: If a partner dissociates and the partnership continues, the partnership must buy out that partner’s partnership interest.
The interest is valued as if the partnership were wound up on the dissociation date.
Value is based on the greater of:
Maya leaves a profitable design firm partnership.
The partnership must buy Maya’s interest.
If partnership continues after partner leaves, buy out the leaving partner.

blocks.text: If a partner dissociates and the partnership continues, the partnership must buy out that partner’s partnership interest.
The interest is valued as if the partnership were wound up on the dissociation date.
Value is based on the greater of:
Maya leaves a profitable design firm partnership.
The partnership must buy Maya’s interest.
If partnership continues after partner leaves, buy out the leaving partner.

sections.title: 49. Dissociated Partner’s Pre-Dissociation Liability
body: A dissociated partner remains liable for obligations incurred before dissociation.
Before Leo leaves, the partnership signs a lease.
Leo remains liable for that lease obligation unless released.
Leaving does not erase old partnership debts.

blocks.text: A dissociated partner remains liable for obligations incurred before dissociation.
Before Leo leaves, the partnership signs a lease.
Leo remains liable for that lease obligation unless released.
Leaving does not erase old partnership debts.

sections.title: 50. Dissociated Partner’s Post-Dissociation Liability
body: A dissociated partner may bind the partnership for up to two years after dissociation if:
The third party reasonably believes the dissociated partner is still a partner;
The third party lacks notice of dissociation; and
The third party is not deemed to know the partner lacks authority.
Filing a statement of dissociation gives notice after 90 days.
This reduces the risk period from two years to 90 days.
A supplier reasonably believes Leo is still a partner and contracts with him.
File dissociation statement to cut off lingering authority.

blocks.text: A dissociated partner may bind the partnership for up to two years after dissociation if:
The third party reasonably believes the dissociated partner is still a partner;
The third party lacks notice of dissociation; and
The third party is not deemed to know the partner lacks authority.
Filing a statement of dissociation gives notice after 90 days.
This reduces the risk period from two years to 90 days.
A supplier reasonably believes Leo is still a partner and contracts with him.
File dissociation statement to cut off lingering authority.

sections.title: 51. Partner’s Power to Bind Partnership
body: A partner can bind the partnership by acting with:
The partnership actually gave the partner authority.
The partner appears to have authority in the ordinary course of partnership business.
Maya is a partner in a bakery.
Maya orders sugar from a supplier.
That is ordinary bakery business, so the partnership is likely bound.
Partner binds partnership in ordinary business unless third party knows partner lacks authority.

blocks.text: A partner can bind the partnership by acting with:
The partnership actually gave the partner authority.
The partner appears to have authority in the ordinary course of partnership business.
Maya is a partner in a bakery.
Maya orders sugar from a supplier.
That is ordinary bakery business, so the partnership is likely bound.
Partner binds partnership in ordinary business unless third party knows partner lacks authority.

sections.title: 52. No Authority If Third Party Knows
body: If the third party knows or receives notice that the partner lacks authority, the partnership is not bound.
“Leo has no authority to buy equipment.”
Leo buys equipment from Supplier anyway.
Apparent authority fails when third party knows the truth.

blocks.text: If the third party knows or receives notice that the partner lacks authority, the partnership is not bound.
“Leo has no authority to buy equipment.”
Leo buys equipment from Supplier anyway.
Apparent authority fails when third party knows the truth.

sections.title: 53. Transfer of Partnership Property
body: Partnership property titled in partnership name may be transferred by a partner signing in the partnership name, unless authority is limited.
Maya signs a deed transferring partnership-owned land in the partnership name.
If she has authority, the transfer works.
If she lacks authority, recovery may depend on what the transferee knew and how title was held.
Partner can transfer partnership property only with proper authority.

blocks.text: Partnership property titled in partnership name may be transferred by a partner signing in the partnership name, unless authority is limited.
Maya signs a deed transferring partnership-owned land in the partnership name.
If she has authority, the transfer works.
If she lacks authority, recovery may depend on what the transferee knew and how title was held.
Partner can transfer partnership property only with proper authority.

sections.title: 54. Statement of Partnership Authority
body: A partnership may file a public statement describing a partner’s authority.
A third party who gives value can rely on a filed grant of authority as conclusive evidence unless the third party actually knows the partner lacks authority.
A filed limitation usually does not give constructive notice except for real property transfers if properly recorded in the real property records.
Statement of authority can prove power, but limits usually matter most for real estate.

blocks.text: A partnership may file a public statement describing a partner’s authority.
A third party who gives value can rely on a filed grant of authority as conclusive evidence unless the third party actually knows the partner lacks authority.
A filed limitation usually does not give constructive notice except for real property transfers if properly recorded in the real property records.
Statement of authority can prove power, but limits usually matter most for real estate.

sections.title: 55. Statement of Denial
body: A partner or person named in a statement may file a statement denying a fact or denying authority.
Leo is named as a partner in a statement but says he is not a partner.
Leo can file a statement of denial.
Statement of denial says, “That authority/status is not true.”

blocks.text: A partner or person named in a statement may file a statement denying a fact or denying authority.
Leo is named as a partner in a statement but says he is not a partner.
Leo can file a statement of denial.
Statement of denial says, “That authority/status is not true.”

sections.title: 56. Partnership Liability for Partner’s Torts
body: A partnership is liable for a partner’s tort if the partner acted:
In the ordinary course of partnership business, or
Maya is a partner in a delivery business.
While making a partnership delivery, Maya negligently hits a pedestrian.
Partnership pays for partner torts in ordinary partnership business.

blocks.text: A partnership is liable for a partner’s tort if the partner acted:
In the ordinary course of partnership business, or
Maya is a partner in a delivery business.
While making a partnership delivery, Maya negligently hits a pedestrian.
Partnership pays for partner torts in ordinary partnership business.

sections.title: 57. Partner Personal Liability
body: Partners are jointly and severally liable for partnership obligations.
A creditor may be able to hold each partner personally responsible for the full obligation.
If partnership assets are insufficient, Supplier may seek payment from partners personally.
General partners personally stand behind partnership debts.

blocks.text: Partners are jointly and severally liable for partnership obligations.
A creditor may be able to hold each partner personally responsible for the full obligation.
If partnership assets are insufficient, Supplier may seek payment from partners personally.
General partners personally stand behind partnership debts.

sections.title: 58. Incoming Partner Liability
body: A new partner is not personally liable for old partnership obligations.
But the new partner’s capital contribution is at risk.
Before Nora joined, the partnership owed $50,000.
Nora is not personally liable for that old debt, but money she contributed to the partnership can be used to pay it.
New partner’s wallet is safe for old debts, but contribution is not.

blocks.text: A new partner is not personally liable for old partnership obligations.
But the new partner’s capital contribution is at risk.
Before Nora joined, the partnership owed $50,000.
Nora is not personally liable for that old debt, but money she contributed to the partnership can be used to pay it.
New partner’s wallet is safe for old debts, but contribution is not.

sections.title: 59. Judgment Against Partnership
body: A judgment against the partnership is not automatically a judgment against the individual partners.
A creditor usually must also get judgment against the partner to reach personal assets.
Even when a partner is liable, the creditor generally must exhaust partnership assets before levying on partner assets.
Creditor may reach partner assets sooner if:
Judgment against partnership alone reaches partnership assets, not automatically partner assets.

blocks.text: A judgment against the partnership is not automatically a judgment against the individual partners.
A creditor usually must also get judgment against the partner to reach personal assets.
Even when a partner is liable, the creditor generally must exhaust partnership assets before levying on partner assets.
Creditor may reach partner assets sooner if:
Judgment against partnership alone reaches partnership assets, not automatically partner assets.

sections.title: 60. Criminal Liability
body: A partnership can be convicted of crimes punishable by fines.
But a partner is not criminally liable merely because they are a partner.
A partner who had no role and no required mental state is not automatically criminally liable.
Partnership membership alone does not create criminal guilt.

blocks.text: A partnership can be convicted of crimes punishable by fines.
But a partner is not criminally liable merely because they are a partner.
A partner who had no role and no required mental state is not automatically criminally liable.
Partnership membership alone does not create criminal guilt.

sections.title: 61. Conversion of Partnership
body: Conversion means a partnership changes legal form.
Conversion = same business changes entity type.

blocks.text: Conversion means a partnership changes legal form.
Conversion = same business changes entity type.

sections.title: 62. Partnership to Limited Partnership
body: A general partnership may convert into a limited partnership.
Unless the agreement says otherwise, all partners must approve.
The partnership files articles of conversion with the state.
A general partner who becomes a limited partner remains liable for obligations incurred before conversion.
The former general partner may still be liable for obligations within 90 days after conversion if a third party reasonably believes the person is still a general partner.
The partnership converts into a limited partnership, and Maya becomes a limited partner.
Maya remains liable for old debts.
Conversion does not erase old general partner liability.

blocks.text: A general partnership may convert into a limited partnership.
Unless the agreement says otherwise, all partners must approve.
The partnership files articles of conversion with the state.
A general partner who becomes a limited partner remains liable for obligations incurred before conversion.
The former general partner may still be liable for obligations within 90 days after conversion if a third party reasonably believes the person is still a general partner.
The partnership converts into a limited partnership, and Maya becomes a limited partner.
Maya remains liable for old debts.
Conversion does not erase old general partner liability.

sections.title: 63. Limited Partnership to General Partnership
body: A limited partnership may convert into a general partnership.
All general and limited partners must approve, despite contrary agreement.
The limited partnership cancels its certificate.
A former limited partner who becomes a general partner is liable as a general partner only for obligations after conversion.
For obligations before conversion, the partner remains liable only as a limited partner.
Former limited partner gets general liability going forward, not backward.

blocks.text: A limited partnership may convert into a general partnership.
All general and limited partners must approve, despite contrary agreement.
The limited partnership cancels its certificate.
A former limited partner who becomes a general partner is liable as a general partner only for obligations after conversion.
For obligations before conversion, the partner remains liable only as a limited partner.
Former limited partner gets general liability going forward, not backward.

sections.title: 64. Merger
body: Merger means two or more entities combine, and only one surviving entity remains.
If a partnership is merging, all partners must approve unless agreement says otherwise.
Legal proceedings continue or substitute surviving entity
Merger = entities combine into one survivor.

blocks.text: Merger means two or more entities combine, and only one surviving entity remains.
If a partnership is merging, all partners must approve unless agreement says otherwise.
Legal proceedings continue or substitute surviving entity
Merger = entities combine into one survivor.

sections.title: 65. Partnership Termination
body: The event that starts the end.
The process of closing the business.
The partnership ends only after winding up is complete.
Dissolution starts the ending; winding up finishes it.

blocks.text: The event that starts the end.
The process of closing the business.
The partnership ends only after winding up is complete.
Dissolution starts the ending; winding up finishes it.

sections.title: 66. Partnership at Will
body: A partnership at will has no fixed end date or specific project.
It dissolves when any partner gives notice of withdrawal.
Maya and Leo run an open-ended consulting partnership.
Leo gives notice that he is leaving.
Partnership at will can end when a partner withdraws.

blocks.text: A partnership at will has no fixed end date or specific project.
It dissolves when any partner gives notice of withdrawal.
Maya and Leo run an open-ended consulting partnership.
Leo gives notice that he is leaving.
Partnership at will can end when a partner withdraws.

sections.title: 67. Partnership for Term or Undertaking
body: This partnership has a fixed time or specific project.
Certain dissociations occur and at least half of remaining partners agree to wind up within 90 days
Maya and Leo form a partnership to develop one app.
When the app project is completed, the partnership dissolves unless they continue.
Term/project partnership ends when term or project ends, unless continued.

blocks.text: This partnership has a fixed time or specific project.
Certain dissociations occur and at least half of remaining partners agree to wind up within 90 days
Maya and Leo form a partnership to develop one app.
When the app project is completed, the partnership dissolves unless they continue.
Term/project partnership ends when term or project ends, unless continued.

sections.title: 68. Dissolution of Any Partnership
body: Agreement says a dissolving event occurred
Business becomes illegal and is not cured within 90 days
Court determines business purpose is frustrated or partnership cannot continue
A transferee seeks judicial winding up when allowed
A partnership’s only business becomes illegal under new law.
If illegality is not cured within 90 days, dissolution occurs.
Any partnership can dissolve by agreement, illegality, court order, or proper transferee action.

blocks.text: Agreement says a dissolving event occurred
Business becomes illegal and is not cured within 90 days
Court determines business purpose is frustrated or partnership cannot continue
A transferee seeks judicial winding up when allowed
A partnership’s only business becomes illegal under new law.
If illegality is not cured within 90 days, dissolution occurs.
Any partnership can dissolve by agreement, illegality, court order, or proper transferee action.

sections.title: 69. Winding Up
body: After dissolution, the partnership continues only to wind up.
Any partner who did not wrongfully dissociate
Legal representative of the last surviving partner
Winding up = closing the business properly.

blocks.text: After dissolution, the partnership continues only to wind up.
Any partner who did not wrongfully dissociate
Legal representative of the last surviving partner
Winding up = closing the business properly.

sections.title: 70. Post-Dissolution Acts
body: After dissolution, the partnership is bound by acts appropriate for winding up.
Maya sells partnership inventory to pay debts.
A partner who knowingly does something inappropriate for winding up may owe damages.
Leo signs a new long-term lease after dissolution, unrelated to winding up.
Leo may be liable for harm caused.
After dissolution, partners can wind up, not start new business.

blocks.text: After dissolution, the partnership is bound by acts appropriate for winding up.
Maya sells partnership inventory to pay debts.
A partner who knowingly does something inappropriate for winding up may owe damages.
Leo signs a new long-term lease after dissolution, unrelated to winding up.
Leo may be liable for harm caused.
After dissolution, partners can wind up, not start new business.

sections.title: 71. Statement of Dissolution
body: A partner who has not wrongfully dissociated may file a statement of dissolution.
After 90 days, it gives third parties notice that partners’ authority is limited.
Statement of dissolution cuts off lingering authority after 90 days.

blocks.text: A partner who has not wrongfully dissociated may file a statement of dissolution.
After 90 days, it gives third parties notice that partners’ authority is limited.
Statement of dissolution cuts off lingering authority after 90 days.

sections.title: 72. Distribution of Assets on Winding Up
body: Partners with positive balances receive liquidating distributions
It first pays bank loans, supplier bills, and partner loans.
Only then do partners split what remains.

blocks.text: Partners with positive balances receive liquidating distributions
It first pays bank loans, supplier bills, and partner loans.
Only then do partners split what remains.

sections.title: 73. Continuation After Dissolution
body: After dissolution but before winding up is complete, partners may agree to continue the business.
Usually, all partners, including properly dissociated partners, must agree.
If they agree, the partnership continues as if dissolution never happened.
Dissolution can be waived before winding up is finished.

blocks.text: After dissolution but before winding up is complete, partners may agree to continue the business.
Usually, all partners, including properly dissociated partners, must agree.
If they agree, the partnership continues as if dissolution never happened.
Dissolution can be waived before winding up is finished.

sections.title: 74. Limited Liability Partnership
body: An LLP is a partnership where partners are generally not personally liable for partnership obligations.
An LLP is formed by filing a statement of qualification with the state.
The name must include something like:
LLP = partnership with limited liability shield.

blocks.text: An LLP is a partnership where partners are generally not personally liable for partnership obligations.
An LLP is formed by filing a statement of qualification with the state.
The name must include something like:
LLP = partnership with limited liability shield.

sections.title: 75. LLP Partner Liability
body: A partner in an LLP is not personally liable for obligations incurred by the LLP.
Maya is a partner in an LLP accounting firm.
Maya is generally not personally liable just because she is a partner.
But if Maya personally committed malpractice, Maya is liable.
LLP protects partners from others’ debts, not from their own misconduct.

blocks.text: A partner in an LLP is not personally liable for obligations incurred by the LLP.
Maya is a partner in an LLP accounting firm.
Maya is generally not personally liable just because she is a partner.
But if Maya personally committed malpractice, Maya is liable.
LLP protects partners from others’ debts, not from their own misconduct.

sections.title: 76. LLP Termination
body: Canceling the statement of qualification turns the LLP back into an ordinary partnership.
It does not automatically dissolve the partnership.
Losing LLP status removes the shield, not the partnership.

blocks.text: Canceling the statement of qualification turns the LLP back into an ordinary partnership.
It does not automatically dissolve the partnership.
Losing LLP status removes the shield, not the partnership.

sections.title: 77. Limited Partnership
body: A limited partnership has at least:
Manages business and has personal liability.
Limited partnership = general partner runs it, limited partner invests.

blocks.text: A limited partnership has at least:
Manages business and has personal liability.
Limited partnership = general partner runs it, limited partner invests.

sections.title: 78. Formation of Limited Partnership
body: A limited partnership is formed by filing a certificate of limited partnership with the state.
Names and addresses of general partners
If no certificate is filed, limited partnership is not formed.

blocks.text: A limited partnership is formed by filing a certificate of limited partnership with the state.
Names and addresses of general partners
If no certificate is filed, limited partnership is not formed.

sections.title: 79. Failed Limited Partnership Formation
body: If someone contributes believing in good faith they are a limited partner, but no limited partnership was formed, they may be liable to third parties who reasonably believed they were a general partner.
Nora invests in what she thinks is a limited partnership.
A supplier reasonably thinks Nora is a general partner.
No valid LP filing can destroy limited liability expectations.

blocks.text: If someone contributes believing in good faith they are a limited partner, but no limited partnership was formed, they may be liable to third parties who reasonably believed they were a general partner.
Nora invests in what she thinks is a limited partnership.
A supplier reasonably thinks Nora is a general partner.
No valid LP filing can destroy limited liability expectations.

sections.title: 80. Limited Partner vs. General Partner
body: Has equal management rights unless agreement says otherwise
General partner manages and risks personal liability. Limited partner invests and usually avoids personal liability.

blocks.text: Has equal management rights unless agreement says otherwise
General partner manages and risks personal liability. Limited partner invests and usually avoids personal liability.

sections.title: 81. Limited Partner Liability to Third Parties
body: A limited partner is usually not personally liable for limited partnership obligations.
Limited partner may be liable if they:
Participate in control of the business and third party reasonably believes they are a general partner.
Nora is a limited partner but negotiates contracts, signs documents, and presents herself as running the business.
A supplier reasonably thinks Nora is a general partner.
Nora may be personally liable to that supplier.
Limited partner loses protection only if they act like a general partner and third party reasonably relies.

blocks.text: A limited partner is usually not personally liable for limited partnership obligations.
Limited partner may be liable if they:
Participate in control of the business and third party reasonably believes they are a general partner.
Nora is a limited partner but negotiates contracts, signs documents, and presents herself as running the business.
A supplier reasonably thinks Nora is a general partner.
Nora may be personally liable to that supplier.
Limited partner loses protection only if they act like a general partner and third party reasonably relies.

sections.title: 82. Limited Partner Safe Harbor Activities
body: Certain activities do not count as control by themselves.
Being officer/director/shareholder of corporate general partner
She attends meetings and votes on selling major assets.
That alone does not make her personally liable.
Limited partners can protect their investment without becoming general partners.

blocks.text: Certain activities do not count as control by themselves.
Being officer/director/shareholder of corporate general partner
She attends meetings and votes on selling major assets.
That alone does not make her personally liable.
Limited partners can protect their investment without becoming general partners.

sections.title: 83. Limited Partner Name in Partnership Name
body: If a limited partner allows their name to be used in the limited partnership’s name, they may be liable to creditors who extend credit without knowing they are only a limited partner.
The LP is called “Nora & Co. Apartments LP.”
Nora is only a limited partner.
A creditor extends credit thinking Nora is a general partner.
Limited partner’s name in business name can create liability.

blocks.text: If a limited partner allows their name to be used in the limited partnership’s name, they may be liable to creditors who extend credit without knowing they are only a limited partner.
The LP is called “Nora & Co. Apartments LP.”
Nora is only a limited partner.
A creditor extends credit thinking Nora is a general partner.
Limited partner’s name in business name can create liability.

sections.title: 84. Limited Partner Withdrawal
body: Unless agreement says otherwise, a limited partner must give six months’ prior written notice to withdraw.
Limited partner withdrawal usually needs advance written notice.

blocks.text: Unless agreement says otherwise, a limited partner must give six months’ prior written notice to withdraw.
Limited partner withdrawal usually needs advance written notice.

sections.title: 85. General Partner Withdrawal from Limited Partnership
body: A general partner may withdraw at any time by giving written notice.
If withdrawal violates the agreement, the general partner may owe damages.
If the only general partner withdraws, the limited partners usually have 90 days to continue and appoint a new general partner.
LP needs a general partner; if sole general partner leaves, limited partners must fix it fast.

blocks.text: A general partner may withdraw at any time by giving written notice.
If withdrawal violates the agreement, the general partner may owe damages.
If the only general partner withdraws, the limited partners usually have 90 days to continue and appoint a new general partner.
LP needs a general partner; if sole general partner leaves, limited partners must fix it fast.

sections.title: 86. Contributions to Limited Partnership
body: A written enforceable promise to contribute is binding.
Nora signs a written promise to contribute $50,000 next month.
Nora must contribute unless properly released.
Contribution can be money, property, services, or enforceable promise.

blocks.text: A written enforceable promise to contribute is binding.
Nora signs a written promise to contribute $50,000 next month.
Nora must contribute unless properly released.
Contribution can be money, property, services, or enforceable promise.

sections.title: 87. Limited Partnership Profits and Losses
body: Partners may allocate profits and losses however they want, if the allocation is in writing.
Profits and losses are allocated based on contributions.
If no written allocation, profits and losses follow those contribution percentages.
LP profits and losses follow written agreement; if none, follow contributions.

blocks.text: Partners may allocate profits and losses however they want, if the allocation is in writing.
Profits and losses are allocated based on contributions.
If no written allocation, profits and losses follow those contribution percentages.
LP profits and losses follow written agreement; if none, follow contributions.

sections.title: 88. Limited Partnership Distributions
body: Partners may agree in writing how distributions are allocated.
If no agreement, distributions follow profits and losses.
A partner usually has no right to distribution before withdrawal or dissolution unless agreement says otherwise.
No automatic early distribution unless agreement gives one.

blocks.text: Partners may agree in writing how distributions are allocated.
If no agreement, distributions follow profits and losses.
A partner usually has no right to distribution before withdrawal or dissolution unless agreement says otherwise.
No automatic early distribution unless agreement gives one.

sections.title: 89. Return of Contribution
body: A partner may have to return a distribution if needed to pay creditors.
One year for return of contribution
Six years if return was wrongful
Distributions may have to come back if creditors are unpaid.

blocks.text: A partner may have to return a distribution if needed to pay creditors.
One year for return of contribution
Six years if return was wrongful
Distributions may have to come back if creditors are unpaid.

sections.title: 90. Assignment of Limited Partnership Interest
body: A limited partnership interest is personal property and can usually be assigned.
Assignment gives money rights, not automatic partner status.

blocks.text: A limited partnership interest is personal property and can usually be assigned.
Assignment gives money rights, not automatic partner status.

sections.title: 91. Limited Partnership Dissolution
body: All general partners and majority of limited distribution rights consent
General partner withdraws and no replacement/continuation within 90 days
Court orders dissolution because business cannot practicably continue
LP dissolves by agreement, time, consent, loss of general partner, or court order.

blocks.text: All general partners and majority of limited distribution rights consent
General partner withdraws and no replacement/continuation within 90 days
Court orders dissolution because business cannot practicably continue
LP dissolves by agreement, time, consent, loss of general partner, or court order.

sections.title: 92. Limited Partnership Winding Up
body: Usually general partners who have not wrongfully dissolved wind up the limited partnership.
If no such general partners exist, limited partners may wind up.
A partner, legal representative, or assignee may ask the court to supervise winding up.
General partners usually wind up; limited partners step in if no general partner can.

blocks.text: Usually general partners who have not wrongfully dissolved wind up the limited partnership.
If no such general partners exist, limited partners may wind up.
A partner, legal representative, or assignee may ask the court to supervise winding up.
General partners usually wind up; limited partners step in if no general partner can.

sections.title: 93. Limited Partnership Distribution of Assets
body: On winding up, assets are distributed:
LP winding up = creditors, unpaid distributions, contributions, then remaining shares.

blocks.text: On winding up, assets are distributed:
LP winding up = creditors, unpaid distributions, contributions, then remaining shares.

sections.title: 94. Limited Partner Derivative Action
body: A limited partner may sue on behalf of the limited partnership if the general partners refuse or demand would likely fail.
Efforts made to get general partners to sue, or
Why making that effort would be useless
The limited partner usually must be a partner:
When the action is brought, and
The recovery goes to the limited partnership.
The limited partner may receive reasonable expenses and attorney’s fees.
Derivative action = limited partner sues for the partnership when managers will not.

blocks.text: A limited partner may sue on behalf of the limited partnership if the general partners refuse or demand would likely fail.
Efforts made to get general partners to sue, or
Why making that effort would be useless
The limited partner usually must be a partner:
When the action is brought, and
The recovery goes to the limited partnership.
The limited partner may receive reasonable expenses and attorney’s fees.
Derivative action = limited partner sues for the partnership when managers will not.

sections.title: Final Partnerships MBE Attack Plan
body: Are there two or more persons?
Are they carrying on a for-profit business?
Step 2: Is This a Formation Problem?
Step 3: Is This an Internal Partner Dispute?
Step 4: Is This About Third-Party Liability?
Did third party know partner lacked authority?
Is the claim contract or tort?
Is dissociated partner liable for old or new obligations?
Step 7: Is It an LLP or Limited Partnership?
Did obligation arise while LLP status existed?
Is person general partner or limited partner?
Did limited partner participate in control?
Did third party reasonably believe limited partner was general partner?

blocks.text: Are there two or more persons?
Are they carrying on a for-profit business?
Step 2: Is This a Formation Problem?
Step 3: Is This an Internal Partner Dispute?
Step 4: Is This About Third-Party Liability?
Did third party know partner lacked authority?
Is the claim contract or tort?
Is dissociated partner liable for old or new obligations?
Step 7: Is It an LLP or Limited Partnership?
Did obligation arise while LLP status existed?
Is person general partner or limited partner?
Did limited partner participate in control?
Did third party reasonably believe limited partner was general partner?

sections.title: Final Memory Sheet
body: Partnership = two or more carrying on for-profit business as co-owners
Specific intent to form partnership not required
Joint ownership alone is not partnership
Gross returns alone are not partnership
Partner by estoppel = representation + reliance + damages
Duty of loyalty = no competing, no adverse dealing, no stealing opportunities
Duty of care = avoid gross negligence, recklessness, intentional misconduct, knowing law violations
Good faith and fair dealing always applies
Partnership agreement controls unless rule is nonwaivable
If silent, profits and losses split equally
If only profits stated, losses follow profits
Partner gets no salary unless agreed or winding up
Transferee gets distribution rights, not management rights
Charging order is creditor’s way to reach partner’s interest
Property bought with partnership assets is presumed partnership property
Partner cannot transfer specific partnership property personally
Each partner has equal management rights
New partner = unanimous consent unless agreement says otherwise
Actual or apparent authority binds partnership
Ordinary business creates apparent authority unless third party knows lack of authority
Partnership liable for partner torts in ordinary business
Partners jointly and severally liable for partnership obligations
Incoming partner not personally liable for old debts
Judgment against partnership is not automatically judgment against partners
Partnership must buy out dissociated partner if partnership continues
Dissociated partner remains liable for old obligations
Statement of dissociation reduces lingering authority risk to 90 days
At-will partnership dissolves when partner withdraws
Term/project partnership dissolves when term/project ends or proper event occurs
Partners can sometimes waive dissolution and continue
LLP partner not personally liable for LLP obligations
Partner still liable for own misconduct
Old obligations before LLP status still matter
Canceling LLP status turns it back into ordinary partnership
LP needs at least one general partner and one limited partner
General partner manages and has personal liability
Limited partner invests and usually has limited liability
Limited partner can lose protection by acting like general partner if third party reasonably believes it
Safe harbor protects many limited partner activities
LP profits/losses follow written agreement; if none, contributions
LP winding up order: creditors, unpaid distributions, contributions, remaining shares
Limited partner may bring derivative action for partnership
